title: depdog language-adapter spec
description: Declares a language's import syntax and module-resolution rules so depdog can analyze it without a hand-written Go adapter. A spec drives a generic comment/string-aware lexer (the same correctness bar as the hand-written scanners), an import-surface extractor, and a common-case resolver that produces the language-neutral core.Graph. Built-in specs ship embedded; user specs are discovered at .depdog/adapters/<name>.yaml.

name.description: The adapter identifier — the --lang value and the label in errors, e.g. "cs" or "lua".

markers.description: Project-root marker files, in priority order. An entry containing '*' is a glob matched against a directory's entries (e.g. "*.csproj"). The nearest ancestor directory holding a marker is the project root.

extensions.description: Source-file extensions to scan, including the dot, e.g. [".rb"] or [".cs"].

skipDirs.description: Directory names pruned from the file walk (in addition to dotdirs, which are always pruned), e.g. ["vendor", "node_modules", "bin", "obj"].

comments.description: The language's comment syntaxes. The lexer never mistakes an import-looking substring inside a comment for an import.

nesting.description: The comment nests (Rust /* */, Elm {- -}): an inner open must be matched before the outer close.

lineAnchored.description: Open/close are only recognised at the very start of a line (Ruby =begin/=end).

strings.description: The language's string/char literal syntaxes. The lexer never mistakes an import-looking substring inside a string for an import.

kind.description: quoted: a normal "..."/'...'/"""...""" literal. char: a single-glyph 'x' literal. raw-hash: Rust r#"..."# with a matched hash level. raw-run: C# raw string of N>=minRun quotes closed by N quotes. Default: quoted.

open.description: Opening delimiter, e.g. "\"", "'", "\"\"\"". For raw-hash this is the leading prefix (e.g. "r").

escape.description: Escape character (usually "\\"); empty means no escapes.

multiline.description: The literal may span newlines (triple-quoted, verbatim).

quoteDoubling.description: A doubled delimiter is a literal delimiter, not a close (C# @"...""...").

quote.description: raw-hash/raw-run: the quote character delimiting the body (e.g. "\"").

minRun.description: raw-run: minimum delimiter run length that opens the literal (C# raw strings: 3).

imports.description: The import surfaces to extract. Each is matched at a word start (like the hand-written scanners' tryRequire).

capture.description: How to read the specifier after the keyword. string: a quoted string literal (require "x"). path-token: a dotted/namespaced identifier chain up to a terminator (using a.b.c;). skip-to-string: skip to a delimiter, then read a string (autoload :C, "x").

kind.description: A label attached to captured refs, referenced by resolve.relativeKinds (e.g. "plain", "relative"). Default: "plain".

separator.description: path-token: the segment separator (".", "::"). The captured specifier is normalised to this separator.

terminator.description: path-token: where the statement ends. Default: newline.

skipTo.description: skip-to-string: the delimiter to skip past before reading the string ("," for Ruby autoload).

prefixKeywords.description: Modifier words that may appear immediately BEFORE the keyword (C# `global using`). The surface still matches when the current word is one of these and the keyword follows.

skipKeywords.description: Modifier words that may appear immediately AFTER the keyword and are skipped before the specifier (C# `using static X`).

alias.description: path-token: a separator token (e.g. "="). When it follows the first token, the specifier captured is the token AFTER it (C# `using X = Y` depends on Y).

strictTerminator.description: path-token: reject the match unless the captured token is immediately followed by the terminator, so a using *statement* (`using (res)`, `using var x = e`) is not read as a directive.

provides.description: A declaration surface that names what a file contributes to the project's name-index (resolve.mode=name-index). E.g. C# `namespace Foo.Bar` declares that this file's directory provides the Foo.Bar namespace.

capture.description: How to read the declared name.

terminator.description: Where the declaration name ends (";" file-scoped, "{" block-scoped).

resolve.description: How a captured specifier is classified (in-module / std / external) and, for in-module, mapped to a graph node directory.

mode.description: path: the specifier is a file path resolved against roots + extensions + index files (Ruby, Lua). name-index: the specifier is a name matched against the set of names project files declare via `provides` (C#, Elm-style). Default: path.

separator.description: The separator in the specifier that maps to a path separator ("/", ".", "::"). Default: "/".

relativeKinds.description: path mode: import surface kinds that resolve against the importing file's directory rather than the roots (Ruby "relative").

roots.description: path mode: project-relative directories a non-relative specifier is resolved against, in order. "." is the project root. Default: ["."].

rootsIfExist.description: path mode: additional roots tried only when they exist on disk (Ruby's conventional "lib" load path).

extensions.description: path mode: file extensions appended to a specifier when it lacks one, e.g. [".rb"]. Default: the top-level extensions.

indexFiles.description: path mode: index-file basenames that make a directory itself importable (Python __init__.py, Lua init.lua).

dropSelfEdges.description: Drop an in-module edge that resolves to the importing file's own directory (Elm). Ruby keeps self-edges. Default: false.

stdlib.description: The standard-library classification table. A specifier that does not resolve in-module and is (or whose head is) in this table classifies as std; anything else is external.

match.description: full: match the whole specifier. head: match the whole specifier, else the head segment before the first separator (Ruby net/http -> net). Default: full.

separator.description: head match: the separator whose first segment is the head ("/").

prefixes.description: Dotted namespace prefixes counted as std (C# "System", "Microsoft" match System.Text, Microsoft.Extensions.*).

builtin.description: Reference a std table bundled with depdog by name, instead of (or in addition to) an inline `modules` list.

tests.description: How test-only source is recognised, so an import seen only in tests is marked TestOnly.

stemSuffixes.description: File-name stem suffixes marking a test file (Ruby ["_test", "_spec"]).

dirs.description: Directory names anywhere in a file's path marking it a test file (Ruby ["spec", "test"]).

module.description: How the graph's ModulePath (the display prefix of every node) is derived.

label.description: Fallback strategy when no manifest name is found. dir-basename: the project root's directory name. Default: dir-basename.

fromFile.description: Read the module name from a manifest by an assignment: find `<recv>.<key> = "value"` (Ruby gemspec spec.name).

glob.description: Glob of the manifest file in the project root, e.g. "*.gemspec".

key.description: The assignment attribute to read, e.g. "name".

commentPrefix.description: Line-comment prefix to ignore in the manifest. Default: "#".
